new_articles.title: Will SK Hynix’s Wall Street debut narrow its 'Korea discount'?
new_articles.summary: SK hynix 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'Will SK Hynix’s Wall Street debut narrow its 'Korea discount'?'이다. As global chip giant SK Hynix makes its Nasdaq debut Friday, the listing serves as a major test of whether the company can shed its long standing "Korea Discount. ". Ji Cheong, associate director at S&P Global Ratings, said that while SK Hynix's IPO will partially support its growing capex, which is forecast at 50 trillion won to 70 trillion won annually over the next two years, the vast majority will be funded through internal cash flow Still, Wong expects the listing will help in strengthening SK Hynix's ability to fund expansion and could pave the way for further U The listing also comes as investors assess whether SK Hynix can maintain its lead in the fast-growing HBM market that underpins AI accelerators Rayliant lead portfolio manager Philip Wool said SK Hynix has become "something of a victim of its own success," as explosive demand for HBM has far outstripped its ability to supply the market Futurum Group's Bulk expects SK Hynix to remain the top HBM supplier, although its market share is likely to decline from roughly 57% last year to around 50% this year before falling into the low-40% range over time as Samsung gains ground and Micron cements itself as the third major player. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

As global chip giant SK Hynix makes its Nasdaq debut Friday, the listing serves as a major test of whether the company can shed its long standing "Korea Discount. ". Ji Cheong, associate director at S&P Global Ratings, said that while SK Hynix's IPO will partially support its growing capex, which is forecast at 50 trillion won to 70 trillion won annually over the next two years, the vast majority will be funded through internal cash flow Still, Wong expects the listing will help in strengthening SK Hynix's ability to fund expansion and could pave the way for further U The listing also comes as investors assess whether SK Hynix can maintain its lead in the fast-growing HBM market that underpins AI accelerators Rayliant lead portfolio manager Philip Wool said SK Hynix has become "something of a victim of its own success," as explosive demand for HBM has far outstripped its ability to supply the market Futurum Group's Bulk expects SK Hynix to remain the top HBM supplier, although its market share is likely to decline from roughly 57% last year to around 50% this year before falling into the low-40% range over time as Samsung gains ground and Micron cements itself as the third major player. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.
